HomePage.heroSubtitle: Instantly check whether human foods are safe for your dog or cat.

fastAnswers.title: Fast Answers
fastAnswers.description: Get clear safety information in seconds, especially in urgent moments.

vetReviewed.title: Vet-Reviewed
vetReviewed.description: Content based on authoritative sources like ASPCA and Pet Poison Helpline.

alwaysFree.title: Always Free
alwaysFree.description: No login required. Access our database anytime, anywhere.

SearchPage.placeholder: Search foods, plants, medications, chemicals, pesticides...

SearchPage.emptyState: Enter a food, plant, medication, chemical, or pesticide above to see if it's safe for your pet.

FoodsPage.title: Food Safety Guide for Dogs & Cats
FoodsPage.description: Browse our vet-reviewed database of human foods and learn what is safe, limited, or toxic for your pets.

PlantsPage.title: Plants
PlantsPage.description: Browse common houseplants, garden plants, and wild plants to see if they are safe for your pets.

EmergencyPage.wizardDescription: Answer a few quick questions to estimate the risk and get guidance on what to do next.

EmergencyBanner.title: Think your pet ate something toxic?

Disclaimer.text: PetPilot provides general information only and is not a substitute for professional veterinary advice. If your pet is sick, injured, or may have eaten something toxic, contact your veterinarian or an animal poison control center immediately.

FoodDetail.subtitle: Find out if {name} is safe for dogs and cats.
FoodDetail.drinkSubtitle: Find out if {name} is safe for dogs and cats to drink.
FoodDetail.safeForDogsDrink: Is {name} Safe for Dogs to Drink?
FoodDetail.safeForCatsDrink: Is {name} Safe for Cats to Drink?
FoodDetail.healthConditionsIntro: Even if {name} is generally safe, it may not be appropriate for pets with certain medical conditions. Always check with your veterinarian if your pet has been diagnosed with any of the following.
FoodDetail.vetsNoteText: PetPilot provides general information for educational purposes. While we reference authoritative veterinary organizations, this page has not been individually reviewed by a veterinarian for your specific pet. Individual animals may react differently based on age, weight, breed, health conditions, and amount consumed. Always consult your veterinarian or a poison control center for personalized advice, especially if your pet is ill, injured, pregnant, nursing, or on medication.
FoodDetail.medicalDisclaimerText: The content on this page is not a substitute for professional veterinary diagnosis, treatment, or emergency care. If you suspect your pet has eaten something harmful, contact your veterinarian or call <aspca>ASPCA Poison Control</aspca> or <pph>Pet Poison Helpline</pph> immediately.

FoodDetail.faqWhatToDoAte: What should I do if my pet ate {name}?
FoodDetail.faqWhatToDoDrank: What should I do if my pet drank {name}?
FoodDetail.faqPreparation: How should {name} be prepared for pets?
FoodDetail.faqAlternatives: What are safe alternatives to {name} for pets?
FoodDetail.faqConditions: Should pets with health conditions avoid {name}?

MedicationsPage.title: Medication Safety Guide for Dogs & Cats
MedicationsPage.description: Browse our vet-reviewed database of human and veterinary medications to learn what is toxic or safe for your pets.

HouseholdChemicalsPage.title: Household Chemical Safety for Dogs & Cats
HouseholdChemicalsPage.description: Browse common household cleaners, automotive products, and other chemicals to see if they are toxic to your pets.

PesticidesPage.title: Pesticide Safety for Dogs & Cats
PesticidesPage.description: Browse insecticides, rodenticides, herbicides, and other pesticides to learn toxicity risks for your pets.

NewsPage.title: Pet Poisoning News
NewsPage.description: Monthly summaries of reported pet poisoning incidents and safety alerts from authoritative sources.
NewsPage.coverageFrom: {count, plural, one {Coverage from # source} other {Coverage from # sources}}
NewsPage.sourceDescription: This summary is based on a report from {source}.
NewsPage.clusterSummaryDescription: This article is part of a larger event. Read the full summary for complete details.
NewsPage.subscribeDescription: New summaries are added monthly. Follow our sitemap for updates.
NewsPage.disclaimerText: These summaries are for pet safety education only and do not replace professional veterinary advice. If your pet may have been exposed to a toxin, contact your veterinarian or a poison control center immediately.

GuidesPage.title: Pet Care Guides
GuidesPage.description: In-depth, vet-reviewed guides on nutrition, safety, wellness, and creating a pet-friendly home.

ToxicityCalculator.title: Quick Toxicity Check
ToxicityCalculator.subtitle: Estimate the risk if your pet ate {name}
ToxicityCalculator.resultCaution: Approaching toxic dose — contact a vet
ToxicityCalculator.resultToxic: Potential poisoning — seek vet care now
ToxicityCalculator.resultUnknown: Risk cannot be calculated from amount alone
ToxicityCalculator.disclaimer: This calculator uses approximate thresholds and is not a substitute for professional veterinary assessment.
ToxicityCalculator.noData: No calculator data is available for {name}. Contact your veterinarian or a poison control center for guidance.

EmergencyWizard.title: Emergency Assessment
EmergencyWizard.description: Quickly assess whether your pet needs immediate veterinary care after eating something potentially harmful.
EmergencyWizard.stepSpecies: Which pet do you need help with?
EmergencyWizard.stepWeight: How much does your pet weigh?
EmergencyWizard.stepSubstance: What did your pet eat or get exposed to?
EmergencyWizard.stepSymptoms: Is your pet showing any of these symptoms?
EmergencyWizard.substanceNoResults: No matching substances found. Try searching the news or contact a vet.
EmergencyWizard.noCalculatorData: No exact dose data is available for this item. Any ingestion of a known toxin should be treated as potentially dangerous.
EmergencyWizard.callNow: Call a poison control center or emergency vet now
EmergencyWizard.resultSafe: Lower risk based on the information provided
EmergencyWizard.resultCaution: Mild to moderate risk — contact a vet
EmergencyWizard.resultToxic: Potential poisoning — seek veterinary care now
EmergencyWizard.resultCritical: Emergency — go to a vet immediately
EmergencyWizard.resultUnknown: Risk cannot be determined from amount alone
EmergencyWizard.resultText_safe: Based on the amount and weight entered, {name} appears to be below common toxic thresholds. Continue to monitor your pet and contact a vet if you are unsure.
EmergencyWizard.resultText_caution: The amount of {name} may be approaching a toxic level for your pet. Contact your veterinarian or a poison control center for guidance.
EmergencyWizard.resultText_toxic: The amount of {name} could be dangerous for your pet. Seek veterinary care or contact a poison control center immediately.
EmergencyWizard.resultText_critical: This may be a life-threatening amount of {name}. Go to the nearest emergency veterinarian or call poison control right now.
EmergencyWizard.resultText_unknown: We don't have enough dose data for {name} to calculate a precise risk. Because this substance is known to be hazardous, contact a veterinarian or poison control center for advice.
EmergencyWizard.disclaimerText: This tool provides general guidance only and is not a substitute for professional veterinary diagnosis or emergency care. If your pet is ill, injured, or may have eaten something toxic, contact your veterinarian or a poison control center immediately.
